senses.definition: relating to the south or the Southern Hemisphere
examples: The austral summer in Antarctica lasts from December to February.
These birds migrate to austral regions during the colder months.
Researchers are studying climate change in the austral oceans.

senses.definition: the former basic unit of money in Argentina, equal to 100 centavos, used from 1985 to 1991
examples: The shop charged fifty australs for the souvenir.
Hyperinflation quickly reduced the value of the austral.
He still keeps an old austral note in his collection.

senses.definition: relating to Australasia or its people, animals, or cultures
examples: The conference focused on Australasian trade policies.
These birds are common in Australasian forests.
Australasian universities attract many international students.

senses.definition: a person from Australasia
examples: Many Australasian tourists visit Southeast Asia each year.
As an Australasian, she feels at home in both Australia and New Zealand.
The team included Europeans and Australasian players.

senses.definition: a hydrocarbon or related chemical substance formerly named with the root australene
examples: The compound was first identified as australene in early studies.
Australene derivatives were tested for their stability.
Researchers compared australene with similar hydrocarbons.

senses.definition: the plural of austral, the former basic unit of money in Argentina
examples: The bill came to three thousand australes.
During inflation, prices rose by millions of australes.
Tourists often found it hard to handle so many australes.

senses.definition: a large country and continent in the southern hemisphere, with its capital in Canberra
examples: She moved to Australia to study at university.
Australia is famous for its beaches and wildlife.
They are planning a trip around Australia next year.

senses.definition: relating to Australia, its people, or its culture
examples: I love Australian music and films.
Australian animals like kangaroos are unique.
She enjoys Australian wine with dinner.

senses.definition: a person from Australia
examples: The Australians were very friendly to us.
As an Australian, he enjoys outdoor sports.
Many Australians travel overseas each year.

senses.definition: a word, phrase, or feature of language that is typical of Australian English
examples: The word "arvo" is a common Australianism meaning afternoon.
Her speech was full of Australianisms that confused foreign listeners.
The dictionary lists many Australianisms used in everyday conversation.

senses.definition: to make something or someone more Australian in style, character, or language
examples: The company tried to Australianize its brand for the local market.
Immigrants often gradually Australianize their accent.
The script was Australianized to appeal to domestic audiences.

senses.definition: the plural of Australian, meaning people from Australia
examples: Australians are known for their relaxed lifestyle.
Many Australians live near the coast.
Australians and visitors enjoyed the festival together.

senses.definition: relating to southern regions of the world, especially the Southern Hemisphere
examples: The australIc fauna shows many unique features.
These plants are typical of australIc environments.
The book describes several australIc climate zones.

senses.definition: relating to an outdated racial classification once applied to some Indigenous peoples of Australia and South Asia
examples: Older texts refer to certain groups as australioid populations.
The term australioid is no longer used in modern anthropology.
She criticized the australioid classification as unscientific.

senses.definition: a person formerly labeled as belonging to the so‑called australioid race, now considered an offensive and outdated term
examples: The museum labels once described local people as australioids.
Modern scholars reject the idea of australioids as a separate race.
The word australioid appears in many colonial reports.

senses.definition: southern, especially used in Latin names for plants and animals found in southern regions
examples: The species Eucalyptus australis is native to southern forests.
They observed Anas australis in the wetlands.
Several plants carry the specific epithet australis.

senses.definition: a type of small glassy meteorite, or tektite, found mainly in Australia
examples: The geologist collected several australite fragments from the field.
Australites are thought to have formed from ancient meteor impacts.
The museum displays an impressive australite specimen.

senses.definition: the plural of australite, small glassy meteorites found especially in Australia
examples: Australites are scattered over a wide area of southern Australia.
Scientists studied the shape of the australites.
The collection includes australites from different locations.

senses.definition: relating to an outdated racial category once used for some Indigenous peoples of Australia and nearby regions
examples: The book describes the now‑abandoned australoid classification.
Scientists no longer use australoid as a serious category.
He criticized the australoid label as racist.

senses.definition: a person formerly described as belonging to the so‑called australoid race, now seen as an offensive and unscientific term
examples: Older researchers referred to such groups as australoids.
The term australoid appears in colonial census records.
Modern texts avoid calling anyone an australoid.

senses.definition: a former scientific subfamily name for a group of early human ancestors including Australopithecus
examples: Australopithecinae were once classified as a separate subfamily.
The fossils of Australopithecinae show a mix of ape‑like and human‑like traits.
Many textbooks now avoid the term Australopithecinae.

senses.definition: relating to Australopithecus, an early group of human ancestors that lived in Africa millions of years ago
examples: Australopithecine fossils were found in East Africa.
The australopithecine skull shows evidence of bipedalism.
Researchers compared australopithecine and modern human bones.

senses.definition: a member of the extinct genus Australopithecus, an early human ancestor
examples: The skeleton belongs to an adult australopithecine.
Australopithecines lived in Africa several million years ago.
Scientists believe australopithecines walked upright.

senses.definition: the plural of australopithecine, referring to several species of the extinct genus Australopithecus
examples: Australopithecines are key to understanding human evolution.
The site has remains of several australopithecines.
Researchers compared different australopithecines across Africa.

senses.definition: a genus of extinct early human ancestors that lived in Africa between about four and two million years ago
examples: Australopithecus is one of the best‑known early human ancestors.
The famous fossil "Lucy" belongs to Australopithecus afarensis.
Australopithecus shows both ape‑like and human‑like features.

senses.definition: a large Australian breed of chicken, usually black, kept mainly for its eggs
examples: They keep a small flock of Australorps in the backyard.
The Australorp is known as an excellent laying hen.
Farmers prize Australorps for their calm nature and good egg production.

senses.definition: the plural of austral, the former Argentine unit of currency
examples: They exchanged their australs for U.S. dollars.
Prices rose into the millions of australs.
He saved several old australs as a reminder of that time.

senses.definition: relating to Austrasia, a region of the early medieval Frankish kingdom in Europe
examples: The Austrasian nobles played a key role in Frankish politics.
Austrasian kings ruled large parts of what is now France and Germany.
He specializes in Austrasian history.

senses.definition: a person from Austrasia, a region in the early Frankish kingdom
examples: Austrasians often fought with their Neustrasian neighbours.
The chronicle mentions several Austrasians by name.
Austrasians and other Franks shared many customs.

senses.definition: a small, mostly mountainous country in central Europe, with its capital in Vienna
examples: They spent their winter holiday in Austria.
Austria is famous for its classical music and alpine scenery.
He comes from Austria but now lives abroad.

senses.definition: relating to Austria, its people, or its culture
examples: He enjoys Austrian food and desserts.
The Austrian countryside is very beautiful.
They attended an Austrian music festival.

senses.definition: a person from Austria
examples: An Austrian showed us around the city.
Many Austrians speak excellent English.
The group included Austrians and Italians.

senses.definition: to make something more Austrian in style, character, or language
examples: The empire tried to Austrianize the administration of new regions.
They Austrianized their menu to attract local customers.
The play was Austrianized for a Vienna audience.

senses.definition: the plural of Austrian, meaning people from Austria
examples: Austrians take pride in their musical heritage.
Many Austrians enjoy skiing in the winter.
Austrians and tourists crowded the main square.

senses.definition: relating to a proposed large family of languages that would include Austroasiatic and Austronesian languages
examples: Some linguists once argued for an Austric language family.
The Austric hypothesis suggests deep links between these languages.
Few scholars now support the Austric grouping.

senses.definition: southern or relating to southern regions, especially in scientific or literary use
examples: The austrine climate supports unique vegetation.
These austrine winds blow from the south.
The study focuses on austrine ecosystems.

senses.definition: a person who keeps, trains, or hunts with hawks, especially goshawks
examples: The austringer carefully trained the young hawk.
As an experienced austringer, she spends hours with her birds.
Medieval illustrators often showed austringers with their hawks on the fist.

senses.definition: the plural of austringer, people who keep or train hawks for hunting
examples: Several austringers attended the falconry meet.
Medieval courts often employed skilled austringers.
The book includes advice for beginner austringers.

senses.definition: a rare name proposed for a chemical element associated with Austria, now unused
examples: Early chemists briefly used the name austrium.
The symbol for austrium never became standard.
Austrium appears only in historical chemical literature.

senses.definition: relating to a family of languages spoken in Southeast Asia and parts of India, including Vietnamese and Khmer
examples: Vietnamese is an Austroasiatic language.
The Austroasiatic family includes many minority languages.
He studies Austroasiatic phonology.

senses.definition: a member language of the Austroasiatic family, or sometimes a speaker of such a language
examples: Several Austroasiatics are endangered.
He compared an Austroasiatic with a neighboring language.
The region is home to many Austroasiatics.

senses.definition: a biogeographical region name used for the Australian area in some older classifications
examples: The fauna of Austrogaea is highly distinctive.
Some authors use the term Austrogaea for the Australian region.
Austrogaea includes Australia and nearby islands in that system.

senses.definition: relating to Austrogaea, the biogeographical region including Australia in some systems
examples: These species are typical of the Austrogaean region.
The Austrogaean flora shows many endemics.
Austrogaean mammals are isolated from other groups.

senses.definition: the practice of trying to tell the future by observing the wind or air currents
examples: In ancient times, priests used austromancy to guide decisions.
Austromancy was one of many forms of divination.
The ritual involved austromancy and reading the clouds.

senses.definition: relating to a large family of languages spoken from Madagascar through Southeast Asia to the Pacific islands
examples: Malay and Tagalog are Austronesian languages.
The Austronesian expansion spread across the Pacific.
She is researching Austronesian grammar.

senses.definition: a person belonging to a people who speak an Austronesian language
examples: Austronesians settled many Pacific islands.
Modern Austronesians live in a wide range of countries.
The archaeologist studied early Austronesians.

senses.definition: a person who especially likes or admires Austria, its people, or its culture
examples: As an austrophil, he travels to Vienna every year.
Many austrophils enjoy Austrian classical music.
The club attracts austrophils from around the world.

senses.definition: a person who greatly likes Austria or Austrian culture
examples: The concert hall was filled with austrophiles.
As an austrophile, she studies the history of the Habsburgs.
The café is popular with local austrophiles.

senses.definition: a strong liking for Austria, its people, or its culture
examples: His austrophilism began after a year in Vienna.
The book reflects a clear austrophilism.
Austrophilism is common among fans of Austrian music.

senses.definition: relating to a humid, forested region in the southeastern United States, used in some biogeographical classifications
examples: Several birds are restricted to the Austroriparian area.
The Austroriparian region has a warm, wet climate.
Austroriparian forests support many amphibians.

senses.definition: a rare or specialized term whose common meaning is unclear in contemporary English
examples: The term ausu appears only in highly technical texts.
Most dictionaries do not list ausu.
Students were unsure how to interpret ausu.

senses.definition: a tropical tree of the Caribbean with very hard wood, especially used in construction and furniture
examples: The carpenter chose ausubo for its strength and durability.
Ausubo grows naturally in Puerto Rican forests.
They exported ausubo logs to foreign markets.

senses.definition: the plural of ausubo, tropical trees valued for their very hard wood
examples: The hillside is covered with mature ausubos.
They carefully managed the ausubos in the reserve.
Ausubos provide high‑quality timber.

senses.definition: a substance made in the body that acts like a local hormone, usually working near where it is produced and for a short time
examples: Histamine is an important autacoid in allergic reactions.
The doctor explained how this autacoid affects blood vessels.
Researchers study autacoids to understand inflammation.

senses.definition: relating to or acting like an autacoid in the body
examples: The drug has autacoidal effects on smooth muscle.
They measured autacoidal activity in the tissue.
Several autacoidal substances are released during injury.

senses.definition: the plural of autacoid, substances made in the body that act locally like short‑acting hormones
examples: Autacoids play a key role in inflammation.
Many drugs work by blocking certain autacoids.
The lecture covered several types of autacoids.

senses.definition: self‑awareness of one’s own bodily sensations or mental states
examples: The philosopher wrote about different forms of autaesthesy.
A high degree of autaesthesy can help in meditation.
Disorders of autaesthesy affect how people feel their own bodies.

senses.definition: describing mineral grains in rocks that have an irregular shape because they grew without forming their own crystal outlines
examples: The rock contains autallotriomorphic quartz grains.
Autallotriomorphic textures suggest a complex cooling history.
They identified autallotriomorphic feldspar under the microscope.

senses.definition: the property of a substance that acts against itself or cancels its own effect
examples: The researcher described a rare case of autantitypy in the reaction.
Autantitypy can limit the overall impact of the compound.
They proposed autantitypy as a possible explanation for the results.

senses.definition: a ruler who has complete power and governs a country alone
examples: The small state was controlled by a ruthless autarch.
Under the autarch, all opposition was suppressed.
The novel tells the story of an aging autarch who fears revolt.
